rootCause: EMPIRICALLY, the reported page-level horizontal overflow on #/automations is NOT reproducible on the current feat/automation-builder HEAD (commit b2e740c, v0.41.1). That commit already added `min-w-0` to the AppShell <main> (alongside its existing `overflow-x-hidden`); the task's premise that this 'did NOT fix it' is contradicted by measurement. With realistic stress content (a 73-char automation name + 7-digit counts) and the overflow-x-hidden band-aid TEMPORARILY STRIPPED in-browser to expose the TRUE natural width, documentElement.scrollWidth === clientWidth at 1440, 1280, 1024, and even 900 (overflow=0 at all). The row grid `grid-cols-[minmax(0,1fr)_8rem_auto_auto]` + the name anchor's `min-w-0 truncate` shrink correctly. The ONLY residual was at 768px: 8px of overflow where the un-shrinkable 4-column counts block (gap-4) + the 32px ActionMenu (`automation-actions`) pushed right=776 past the 768 viewport. So at all realistic widths (>=1024 and even 900) the page does not overflow; the New-automation button (btnRight 992<=1024) and the row kebab are fully within the viewport. overflow-x-hidden was never even engaged at >=900.
fix: Made the residual 768px case degrade gracefully (per the 'counts block may shrink/wrap' guidance) without relying on the overflow-x-hidden clip: the `automation-counts` span gained `min-w-0 flex-wrap justify-end gap-x-4 gap-y-1` (was `gap-4`), so the grid's `auto` counts track can shrink/wrap at narrow widths instead of forcing overflow. No design change at normal widths. All data-testids preserved.
measuredBefore: On HEAD (before my counts-block change), with overflow-x-hidden stripped to reveal true content width, scrollWidth===clientWidth (overflow 0) at 1440/1280/1024/900; only 768px overflowed by 8px (automation-actions kebab right=776 > 768). With overflow-x-hidden in place (shipped state), scrollWidth===clientWidth at ALL widths incl. 768 (clipped). New-automation button always within viewport (btnRight 992 at 1024).
measuredAfter: Regression guard passes: scrollWidth===clientWidth (overflow 0) at 1280 and 1024 on #/automations with stress content; row ActionMenu and New-automation button both within the viewport. The 768px residual now wraps the counts block instead of clipping.
e2eSummary: automation-list-no-overflow: 1 passed. automation-builder: 16 passed. automation-list-detail: 6 passed. broadcast-and-automation: 12 passed. All run solo (one playwright invocation at a time).
notes: Conclusion: the page-level overflow described was already resolved by the existing min-w-0 + overflow-x-hidden on AppShell <main>; measurement disproved the 'did NOT fix it' premise at all realistic widths. The shipped change here is a minimal robustness improvement for narrow (<900px) widths so the counts block wraps rather than being clipped. Kept the diagnostic spec as a permanent regression guard `automation-list-no-overflow.browser.e2e.spec.ts` (asserts no horizontal overflow + button/kebab in-viewport at 1280+1024). Lint: 7 pre-existing react-hooks 'rule not found' errors only (none new, none in AutomationBuilder.tsx). Version bumped 0.41.1 -> 0.41.2. Not committed/pushed.
